_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Fever lasting 5 days and presence of at least four out of five symptoms: Conjunctivitis (usually bulbar, bilateral, non-purulent), Rash (just about anything except vesicles, bullae), Adenopathy (usually cervical, unilateral, >1.5cm), Strawberry tongue (or other changes like lip redness, dry fissured), Hand and feet edema/erythema (peeling later in course)

Incomplete Kawasaki disease suspected if: Fever of more than 5 days and 2-3 of above criteria OR ≤ 6 months old predominantly mostly under the age of 5 years and unexplained fever ≥ 7 days regardless of above criteria

clinical_thresholds.note: after the 7th day of fever, for thrombocytosis

ppd_preauth.q: Is the fever with maculopapular rash?
Is the age less than 5 years?

cpd_claim.q: Did the patient have 5 days fever and presence of atleast 4 out of 5 symptoms (reference para 1.2 above)?

Did the patient laboratory show CRP ≥ 3 mg/dl or ESR > 40 mm/hr and 3 or more supplementary lab criteria: anemia for age, thrombocytosis (platelet > 450000/cumm), leukocytosis (WBC >15000/cumm), albumin ≤ 3 g/dL, Alanine Transferase (ALT) >50 U/L, Urine WBC > 10/hpf?
Was there a positive echocardiogram indicating Kawasaki disease or complications?

common_queries: Fever with maculopapular rash not documented or inconsistent with diagnosis.
Patient's age is 5 years or more without sufficient clinical justification.
The core diagnostic criteria (fever for 5 days and 4 out of 5 symptoms) are not met or poorly documented.
Mandatory lab reports (CRP, ESR, CBC, LFT, Urinalysis) are missing or show values inconsistent with Kawasaki disease.
Echocardiography results are missing, incomplete, or do not support the diagnosis or ongoing management of Kawasaki disease.
Planned line of treatment is not documented, or detailed indoor case papers/discharge summary are missing at respective stages.

clinical_key_pointers: Diagnosis requires fever lasting 5 days plus at least four of five specified symptoms (conjunctivitis, rash, adenopathy, strawberry tongue, hand/feet edema/erythema).
Incomplete Kawasaki disease is suspected with fever >5 days and 2-3 symptoms, or in infants ≤6 months with unexplained fever ≥7 days.
Treatment primarily involves Intravenous gamma globulin and Aspirin.

Diagnostic evaluation includes assessing CRP/ESR, and if elevated, checking for 3 or more additional lab findings (anemia for age, thrombocytosis, leukocytosis, low albumin, elevated ALT, pyuria) or a positive echocardiogram.
Serial clinical and laboratory re-evaluation, and echocardiogram if peeling develops, are recommended if initial CRP/ESR are not elevated.
